name: Cannoli Siciliana
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour and salt into a bowl. Make a well in the center and add the Marsala wine and sugar. Work the ingredients into the center until you have a firm dough that is slightly sticky.
2. Place the dough on a lightly floured pastry cloth or board and knead vigorously for about 15 minutes, until it is smooth with no dry spots.
3. Form the dough into a ball, wrap in a clean, dampened cloth, and let rest for 2 hours.
4. Roll out the rested dough on the pastry cloth or board to be no more than 1/16 inch thick. Cut into 5-inch squares.
5. Shape each square into a diamond around the metal cannoli tube (place one point toward you, then place the tube vertically across the middle at the points). Fold the sides over the tube, moistening the overlap with water and pressing together.
6. Place the filled tubes into deep fat preheated to 375°F. Remove when golden brown and drain on absorbent paper.
7. Fill a pastry bag fitted with a large plain tube (or use the nozzle without a tube) with the ricotta filling. Pipe the filling into the cooled shells.
Makes 12 cannoli.
